C:
Here's a description built so the first three lines do the heavy lifting (that's all most people see before the "more" fold):

---

**Stay consistent without the busywork. Build habits that survive a packed calendar.**

Designed for people who don't have time to manage their habit tracker. Check in with one tap, between meetings, and let the app handle the rest.

No streak guilt. No 20-step setup. Just the few habits that actually move your day — tracked in seconds and built around a schedule that never sits still.

**Why busy professionals stick with it:**
- One-tap check-ins — log a habit in under three seconds
- Smart reminders that read your calendar and stay quiet during back-to-backs
- Flexible streaks that forgive the occasional travel day instead of punishing it
- A clean weekly view that shows progress at a glance, no chart-reading required
- Private by default — your routines stay on your device

Start with one habit. Keep it for a week. That's the whole pitch.

---

Two things worth deciding before you ship it: the bracketed claims (calendar integration, on-device privacy) need to be true of your actual build — drop the bullet if the feature isn't there. And if you have a named hook for the app, swap it into the opening line, since a branded promise tends to outperform a generic one in the first slot.
